Title: Yerba mate (Ilex paraguariensis) genome provides new insights into convergent evolution of caffeine biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stimulant xanthine alkaloid; specialized metabolite in caffeinated beverages
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Caffeine biosynthesis / xanthine alkaloid methylation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Primarily methylates xanthine to form 3-methylxanthine; also shows secondary activity with 7-methylxanthine to produce theobromine in vitro
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an upstream intermediate leading to caffeine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes caffeine biosynthesis by catalyzing the first committed methylation step from xanthine toward 3-methylxanthine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest expression in caffeine-accumulating tissue; mature leaves higher than roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest expression in caffeine-accumulating tissue; mature leaves higher than roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome mining; phylogenetic analysis; tissue expression profiling; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; radiochemical enzyme assays; HPLC product identification; kinetic analysis; molecular docking
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing/assembly ENA PRJEB65927; Illumina-only assembly ENA PRJEB36685; transcriptome/RNA-seq PRJNA315513; SRP043293; SRP110129
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme for engineering de novo caffeine production when stacked with IpCS2 and IpCS3
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of three recently tandemly duplicated CS-type genes; no XMT-type caffeine-producing enzymes were found in yerba mate; docking suggests W156, N221, and Y265 help position xanthine for N3 methyl transfer
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IpCS1 is one of three recently duplicated CS-type SABATH genes highly expressed in caffeine-accumulating yerba mate tissues. 2. Recombinant IpCS1 primarily methylates xanthine to produce 3-methylxanthine, defining the first step of the proposed yerba mate caffeine pathway. 3. Together with IpCS2 and IpCS3, IpCS1 enables a complete X→3X→TB→CF route distinct from the coffee/tea pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stimulant xanthine alkaloid; specialized metabolite in caffeinated beverages
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Caffeine biosynthesis / xanthine alkaloid methylation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes caffeine biosynthesis by converting 3-methylxanthine to theobromine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest expression in caffeine-accumulating tissue; mature leaves higher than roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest expression in caffeine-accumulating tissue; mature leaves higher than roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome mining; phylogenetic analysis; tissue expression profiling; RT-PCR cloning; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; radiochemical enzyme assays; HPLC product identification; kinetic analysis; molecular docking
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing/assembly ENA PRJEB65927; Illumina-only assembly ENA PRJEB36685; transcriptome/RNA-seq PRJNA315513; SRP043293; SRP110129
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful middle-pathway enzyme for caffeine metabolic engineering in combination with IpCS1 and IpCS3
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Docking suggests S24 and T25 contribute to positioning 3-methylxanthine; part of the recent tandem duplication that underlies caffeine evolution in Ilex
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IpCS2 is a duplicated CS-type SABATH gene expressed in caffeine-producing tissue. 2. Recombinant IpCS2 shows activity only with 3-methylxanthine to form theobromine, identifying the second step in the proposed pathway. 3. IpCS2 channels flux from 3X to TB in the convergently evolved yerba mate caffeine pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stimulant xanthine alkaloid; specialized metabolite in caffeinated beverages
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Caffeine biosynthesis / xanthine alkaloid methylation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Theobromine to caffeine terminal methylation branch
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Theobromine methyltransferase / caffeine synthase-type SABATH methyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SABATH methyltransferase family; Rossmann-like fold methyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Preferentially methylates theobromine to form caffeine; can also methylate paraxanthine to caffeine in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly forms caffeine from theobromine and completes the yerba mate caffeine pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest expression in caffeine-accumulating tissue; mature leaves higher than roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest expression in caffeine-accumulating tissue; mature leaves higher than roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome mining; phylogenetic analysis; tissue expression profiling; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; radiochemical enzyme assays; HPLC product identification; kinetic analysis; X-ray crystallography (PDB 8UZD); structural comparison; molecular docking
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing/assembly ENA PRJEB65927; Illumina-only assembly ENA PRJEB36685; transcriptome/RNA-seq PRJNA315513; SRP043293; SRP110129; protein structure PDB 8UZD
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key terminal enzyme for caffeine engineering; structural knowledge can guide protein engineering for altered methylxanthine specificity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Crystal structure revealed convergent substrate-positioning solution relative to coffee CcDXMT despite different active-site residues and substrate orientation; residues implicated in TB preference include A22, R23, T25, H221, and Y265 evolutionary changes; product complex contained SAH and caffeine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IpCS3 is the terminal caffeine-forming SABATH enzyme in yerba mate, preferentially converting theobromine to caffeine. 2. X-ray crystal structure (8UZD) showed a Rossmann-like SABATH fold and convergent active-site chemistry enabling N1 methylation of theobromine. 3. IpCS3, together with IpCS1 and IpCS2, establishes the complete X→3X→TB→CF pathway in yerba mate.
